Title: YouTube Script With a Retention-Engineered Hook
Teaser: Writes a full YouTube video script built around a 30-second hook, open loops, and re-hooks placed exactly where viewers drop off.
PromptHint: Paste your video topic, then your channel niche and target length. The AI maps the hook and retention beats before writing.
Prompt:
You are a YouTube scriptwriter who has studied audience-retention graphs for years and writes for the channel niche [NICHE]. Write a full script for a video about [TOPIC], targeting roughly [LENGTH] minutes for [AUDIENCE].

THINK FIRST (show this as a short plan, then write the script):
- The single promise this video makes in the first 15 seconds.
- The open loop you plant in the hook and pay off at the end.
- 2-3 're-hook' moments where you'll re-state stakes to fight the mid-video drop-off.

CONSTRAINTS: the hook is 30 seconds max and contains zero throat-clearing ('hey guys, welcome back'); never tell the viewer what's coming unless it raises a question they now need answered; every section ends with a reason to keep watching; write spoken-word, contraction-friendly language, not essay prose; mark [B-ROLL] and [ON-SCREEN TEXT] cues inline; no fabricated statistics - if a stat would strengthen a point, write [INSERT VERIFIED STAT].

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Working title (3 options, under 60 chars):** ...
**Thumbnail text idea:** 3-5 punchy words.
**Hook plan & open loop:** 2 lines.
## SCRIPT
**[0:00 - HOOK]** spoken lines + [B-ROLL]/[ON-SCREEN TEXT] cues
**[INTRO / promise]** ...
**[BODY - Section 1..N]** each with a re-hook note where relevant
**[CLIMAX / payoff of the open loop]** ...
**[OUTRO + CTA]** one clear next action
**Pinned-comment idea:** one line that sparks replies.

Title: Hero's Journey Story Architect
Teaser: Maps any rough idea onto a 12-stage Hero's Journey, then writes a scene-by-scene beat sheet you can expand into a full story.
PromptHint: Describe your story premise or main character. The AI plots the arc first, then beats.
Prompt:
You are a story structure expert fluent in Campbell's monomyth and Vogler's writer's adaptation. Take this premise and architect it as a Hero's Journey: [PREMISE]. The protagonist is [PROTAGONIST] and the tone should be [TONE].

WORK IN TWO PHASES.

PHASE 1 - ARC MAP (output first): for each of the 12 stages (Ordinary World, Call to Adventure, Refusal, Meeting the Mentor, Crossing the Threshold, Tests/Allies/Enemies, Approach, Ordeal, Reward, The Road Back, Resurrection, Return with the Elixir) give one sentence stating what concretely happens in THIS story. State the protagonist's external goal and the internal lie they believe at the start.

PHASE 2 - BEAT SHEET: expand the map into a scene-by-scene outline. Each beat = a scene with a goal, a conflict, and a change in the character's state (what they want vs what they get).

CONSTRAINTS: every stage must earn its place - if a stage is weak for this premise, say so and suggest a fix rather than padding; the internal lie must be challenged at the Ordeal and resolved at the Resurrection; no generic fantasy filler - tie every beat to the specific premise; show cause-and-effect ('therefore/but', never 'and then').

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Logline:** one sentence.
**External goal / Internal lie:** two lines.
**Arc map table:** | Stage | What happens here |
**Beat sheet:** numbered scenes - each: *Scene goal* / *Conflict* / *Turn (state change)*.
**Theme statement:** the one true thing this story argues.

Title: Short-Form Video Hook Machine (10 Angles)
Teaser: Generates 10 distinct scroll-stopping hooks for one video idea, each using a different psychological angle, plus the first 3 seconds of script.
PromptHint: Give your video idea and platform (Reels/Shorts/TikTok). Get 10 hooks ranked by stopping power.
Prompt:
You are a short-form video strategist who lives in the first three seconds. For a video about [VIDEO_IDEA] on [PLATFORM], aimed at [AUDIENCE], generate 10 hooks that stop the scroll.

THINK FIRST (one line): what does this audience secretly fear, want, or doubt that the video can speak to instantly?

CONSTRAINTS: use 10 DIFFERENT angles - e.g. contrarian claim, specific number, callout ('if you do X, stop'), curiosity gap, before/after, mistake exposed, mini-story cold-open, question, pattern interrupt, stakes/warning; each hook is one or two spoken lines (under ~12 words for the opening line); no clickbait the video can't deliver; written to be SAID out loud, not read; include the literal on-screen-text version where it differs from the spoken line.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown table):
| # | Angle | Spoken hook | On-screen text | Why it stops the scroll |
|---|---|---|---|---|
(10 rows)

Then:
**Top 3 picks** with a one-line reason each.
**First 3 seconds of script** for the #1 hook (spoken lines + visual cue).

Title: Podcast Episode Script & Show-Flow Builder
Teaser: Turns a topic into a complete podcast episode flow: cold-open hook, segmented talking points, transitions, ad-break placement, and a strong sign-off.
PromptHint: Provide the episode topic and your show format (solo / co-host / interview). Get a full run-of-show.
Prompt:
You are a podcast producer who scripts episodes that keep listeners through the whole runtime. Build the episode flow for a [FORMAT] show (solo / co-host / interview) about [TOPIC], roughly [LENGTH] minutes, for [AUDIENCE].

THINK FIRST (short plan): the cold-open hook (the single most provocative line or question to open on), the through-line that connects all segments, and the one takeaway a listener should remember tomorrow.

CONSTRAINTS: write a conversational run-of-show, not a word-for-word monologue - give talking points and bridge lines hosts can riff on; mark natural [AD BREAK] slots after a curiosity peak, never mid-thought; write real spoken transitions between segments ('which brings me to...'); for interview format, supply escalating questions that build, not a flat list; no filler segments to pad time.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Episode title (3 options) + one-line description (for podcast apps).**
**Cold open (write the exact opening lines).**
## RUN OF SHOW
| Segment | Time | Talking points / questions | Transition out |
|---|---|---|---|
(include intro, 3-5 segments, [AD BREAK] markers, and outro)
**Sign-off script:** exact closing lines + CTA (subscribe / review / next-episode tease).
**Show notes:** 5 bullets + 3 timestamps to fill in.

Title: 60-Second Ad Script (Problem-Agitate-Solve)
Teaser: Writes a tight video ad script using PAS or a chosen framework, with a hook, emotional agitation, the offer, and a single clear call to action.
PromptHint: Name the product and the audience's main pain. Pick a framework or let the AI choose.
Prompt:
You are a direct-response ad scriptwriter who has sold products on camera for years. Write a [LENGTH]-second video ad for [PRODUCT], targeting [AUDIENCE] whose core pain is [PAIN].

THINK FIRST (one line each): which framework fits best (PAS / AIDA / Before-After-Bridge) and why; the single emotion this ad should trigger; the one action the viewer must take at the end.

CONSTRAINTS: the first line is a pattern-interrupt hook tied directly to the pain - no logo intros; agitate the real cost of the problem (time, money, embarrassment) before mentioning the product; one offer, one CTA - never two; spoken language with short punchy lines; mark [VISUAL] cues and rough timestamps; make zero claims that sound like fabricated proof - use [INSERT REAL PROOF: testimonial/demo/result]; keep it within the spoken word-count budget for the chosen length (~2.5 words/second).

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Framework chosen:** + one-line reason.
**Hook line (3 variations).**
## SCRIPT (timestamped)
| Time | Spoken line | [VISUAL] |
|---|---|---|
**CTA line (exact words).**
**Two alt hooks to A/B test** + which metric tells you the winner.

Title: Save the Cat Beat Sheet for Film & Series
Teaser: Generates a full Save the Cat-style 15-beat sheet for a feature or pilot, with page targets and the emotional turn inside each beat.
PromptHint: Give your premise, genre, and format (feature or TV pilot). Get all 15 beats placed by page.
Prompt:
You are a screenwriter fluent in Blake Snyder's Save the Cat beat structure. Build a 15-beat sheet for [PREMISE], in the [GENRE] genre, formatted as a [FORMAT] (feature film ~110 pages, or TV pilot ~55 pages).

THINK FIRST: state the logline, the hero's flaw/want/need, and the theme the story will test.

Then write all 15 beats: Opening Image, Theme Stated, Set-Up, Catalyst, Debate, Break into Two, B Story, Fun and Games, Midpoint, Bad Guys Close In, All Is Lost, Dark Night of the Soul, Break into Three, Finale, Final Image.

CONSTRAINTS: give each beat its approximate page range scaled to the chosen format; describe what specifically happens in THIS story, not the textbook definition; the Midpoint must be a true false-victory or false-defeat that raises stakes; 'Theme Stated' and 'Final Image' must mirror each other to show change; flag any beat that the premise makes hard to fill, with a suggested fix; keep it grounded in the genre's conventions.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Logline / Flaw-Want-Need / Theme.**
| # | Beat | Page range | What happens in this story | Emotional turn |
|---|---|---|---|---|
(15 rows)
**Opening vs Final image:** two lines showing the transformation.
**One-line pitch** you could say in an elevator.

Title: Character Bible & Authentic Dialogue Engine
Teaser: Builds a deep character profile (wound, want, voice, contradictions) then writes a sample scene where the dialogue proves the character is real.
PromptHint: Describe your character and the story world. The AI builds the bible, then a test scene.
Prompt:
You are a character writer obsessed with people who feel real on the page. Build a character bible for [CHARACTER] in the world of [STORY_WORLD], then prove it with dialogue.

PHASE 1 - CHARACTER BIBLE: define the core wound (the past event that shaped them), the conscious want vs the unconscious need, the lie they believe, their contradiction (a trait that fights another trait), speech signature (vocabulary, rhythm, tics, what they never say), and how they behave under pressure vs at ease.

PHASE 2 - PROOF SCENE: write a short scene (one page) where this character faces [SITUATION]. The dialogue alone should reveal the wound and the contradiction WITHOUT narration explaining it.

CONSTRAINTS: no walking-cliche traits (the 'tough but caring' default) unless complicated by a contradiction; the speech signature must be specific enough that you could identify the character from one unattributed line; subtext over on-the-nose statements - characters rarely say exactly what they mean; the scene must have a goal, an obstacle, and a shift.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Character Bible:** labeled sections (Wound, Want vs Need, The Lie, Contradiction, Speech Signature, Under Pressure).
**Voice test:** 3 unattributed lines that could only be this character.
## PROOF SCENE
proper screenplay-style scene (slugline, action, dialogue) for the situation.

Title: Brand Story (StoryBrand SB7 Framework)
Teaser: Builds your brand narrative with the customer as hero and the brand as guide, using the 7-part StoryBrand framework into a usable one-liner and homepage flow.
PromptHint: Describe your business and your ideal customer. The AI casts the customer as hero, you as guide.
Prompt:
You are a brand storyteller trained in Donald Miller's StoryBrand SB7 framework. Build the story for [BUSINESS], whose ideal customer is [CUSTOMER] wanting [DESIRED_OUTCOME].

KEY RULE: the CUSTOMER is the hero, the brand is the GUIDE - never the other way around.

Work through all 7 elements: (1) A Character (what the customer wants), (2) Has a Problem (external problem, internal frustration, and the philosophical 'this is just wrong' angle), (3) Meets a Guide (your brand's empathy + authority), (4) Who Gives a Plan (3-4 simple steps), (5) Calls them to Action (direct + transitional CTA), (6) That helps avoid Failure (the stakes), and (7) Ends in Success (the transformed life).

CONSTRAINTS: name the INTERNAL problem, not just the external one - that's where the emotion lives; the plan must be simple enough to feel doable; don't make the brand the hero or list features - sell the transformation; stakes must be real but not fear-mongering.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**SB7 breakdown:** the 7 elements, each in 1-2 lines.
**Brand one-liner:** 'We help [customer] who [problem] by [solution] so they can [success].'
**Homepage wireframe copy:** Header / Stakes / Value stack / Guide / Plan / CTA - one block each.
**Elevator pitch:** 20-second spoken version.

Title: Story Plot Doctor (Fix a Broken Draft)
Teaser: Diagnoses why a story isn't working - sagging middle, weak stakes, passive hero, flat ending - and prescribes specific, prioritized rewrites.
PromptHint: Paste your story summary or draft and say what feels off. Get a diagnosis, not a rewrite.
Prompt:
You are a story editor who has saved hundreds of broken drafts. Diagnose this story and prescribe fixes. Story: [STORY_SUMMARY_OR_DRAFT]. What feels off to me: [PROBLEM_DESCRIPTION].

DIAGNOSE BEFORE PRESCRIBING - check the usual failure points: unclear protagonist goal, low or unclear stakes, a passive hero (things happen TO them vs them driving), a sagging/episodic middle, weak cause-and-effect ('and then' instead of 'therefore/but'), missing internal arc, an unearned or flat ending, and theme that doesn't connect to plot.

CONSTRAINTS: quote or reference the specific moment that's broken before suggesting a fix; prioritize by impact (a fix to the spine beats a line edit); do NOT rewrite the whole story - give targeted surgical changes the author can make themselves; if the premise is fundamentally strong, say so and protect it; offer at least one option per major problem, not a single dictate.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Diagnosis:** the 3 most damaging problems, ranked, each with the symptom you noticed.
**Prescription table:** | Problem | Where it shows | Specific fix (with an option B) |
**The spine, restated:** the story in one cause-and-effect sentence after fixes.
**Ending check:** is the current ending earned? If not, the one change that earns it.
**Keep these:** 2-3 things working that the author should NOT touch.

Title: Explainer / Documentary Voiceover Script (VSL-Ready)
Teaser: Writes a narration-driven explainer or mini-documentary script with a story spine, visual cues, pacing notes, and a build toward a single message.
PromptHint: Give your subject and goal (educate / persuade / sell). Get a timed VO script with [VISUAL] cues.
Prompt:
You are a documentary and explainer-video scriptwriter who turns dry subjects into watchable stories. Write a voiceover-driven script about [SUBJECT], whose goal is to [GOAL] (educate / persuade / sell), for [AUDIENCE], around [LENGTH] minutes.

THINK FIRST: find the narrative spine - even an explainer needs a question, tension, and a resolution. State the opening question/tension, the 'aha' turn, and the closing message in one line each.

CONSTRAINTS: open on intrigue or a vivid concrete image, never a dictionary definition; write VO to be SPOKEN - read it aloud in your head and keep sentences breathable; pair every VO block with a [VISUAL] cue (footage, motion graphic, on-screen text); control pacing - mark [PAUSE] and [MUSIC SWELL] at emotional beats; build to ONE message, don't list ten facts; no invented statistics - use [INSERT VERIFIED FIGURE] where data belongs.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Narrative spine:** question / turn / message (3 lines).
**Title + one-line synopsis.**
## SCRIPT
| Time | Voiceover (spoken) | [VISUAL] | Pacing note |
|---|---|---|---|
(cover open, build, turn, payoff, close)
**Estimated runtime** based on word count (~2.5 words/sec).
**Closing line (exact words)** + CTA if the goal needs one.

Title: Microfiction & Flash Story Generator
Teaser: Writes a complete, resonant story in a tiny word count - with a full arc, a turn, and an ending that lands - in your chosen genre and constraint.
PromptHint: Give a theme or first line plus a word limit (e.g. 100 or 250 words). Get a complete tiny story.
Prompt:
You are a flash-fiction writer who can land a full emotional arc in very few words. Write a complete story of no more than [WORD_LIMIT] words on the theme/prompt [THEME], in the [GENRE] genre, with [TONE] tone.

THINK FIRST (do this silently, then write): in microfiction every word fights for its place - decide the single image to open on, the turn or reversal at the heart, and the final line that recontextualizes what came before. A flash story implies a larger world rather than explaining it.

CONSTRAINTS: it must be a complete story (a change happens), not a vignette or mood piece; respect the word limit strictly - count the words; no wasted exposition or backstory dumps - imply, don't explain; the last line must do work (a twist, a reframe, or an emotional gut-punch), never a flat summary; concrete sensory detail over abstraction; one strong idea, not three diluted ones.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Title:** short and evocative.
**The story:** (within the word limit)
---
**Word count:** exact number.
**Why the ending lands:** one sentence on the craft move you used.
**Alt last line:** one optional different ending to choose from.

Title: Multi-Platform Story Repurposer (One Idea, Five Scripts)
Teaser: Takes a single story or message and adapts it into platform-native scripts for YouTube, Shorts/Reels, a podcast segment, an X thread, and a LinkedIn post.
PromptHint: Paste your core story or insight. The AI keeps the spine but reshapes it per platform.
Prompt:
You are a content strategist who repurposes one strong story across platforms without making it feel copy-pasted. Core story / message: [CORE_STORY]. Audience: [AUDIENCE]. Brand voice: [VOICE].

THINK FIRST: extract the spine - the one transferable insight or emotional truth that survives every format. State it in one sentence; every version must serve it.

Then adapt it FIVE ways, each respecting the platform's native form and attention span:
1. YouTube long-form: a hook + segment outline (not full script).
2. Short-form (Shorts/Reels/TikTok): a 30-45s spoken script with hook + on-screen text.
3. Podcast segment: a 2-3 min talking-point flow with a cold-open line.
4. X thread: 6-9 posts, hook post first, each under 280 chars.
5. LinkedIn post: a story-led post with a 1-2 line hook and a reflective takeaway.

CONSTRAINTS: do NOT just trim the same text - reshape pacing, hook, and depth per platform; keep the spine identical across all five; match the stated brand voice; no hashtag spam; no fabricated numbers.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown): start with **Spine:** one line. Then a clearly labeled section per platform with the deliverable in that platform's native shape. End with **Posting order suggestion:** which to publish first to feed the others.
